title: Improved mapping fields
excerpt:
The existing dynamic mapping is quite limiting in its usefulness. 
We can only bring data in if its a trigger, the data types are limited, column types are limited, we don’t even know what column the data is actually com…

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml:
The existing dynamic mapping is quite limiting in its usefulness.

We can only bring data in if its a trigger, the data types are limited, column types are limited, we don’t even know what column the data is actually coming from or going to - so we can’t query the column in any way ourselves.

We need a new mapping mechanism where we can define fields and allowed column types (rather than data types and monday saying what columns we can use). Here are some example types describing what I’d like to see.

{
direction: "inbound" | "outbound",
mappable_fields: {id: string, title: string, column_types: ColumnType[], required?: boolean}[]
}

runtime value sent:

{
direction: "inbound" | "outbound"
mapped_fields: {id: string, column_id: string, column_type: ColumnType}[]
}

We query for the values ourselves, mutate them ourselves. No reliance on monday to get the values or create the item (or update an existing item which we can’t do now!). This is just a simple definition of allowed mappings, and lets developers do with it as they need.

Can these also be statically defined in our custom fields (workflow and integrations sentences)? Often times we know what the fields will be that we need mapped in advance - there is no reason to have to use a custom URL for what amounts to a static mapping.
